steps.title: Understand what the question is asking
steps.content: The question asks which finding from the data would challenge the assumption that snakes cannot detect sound traveling through the air. To challenge that assumption, the finding must show snakes CAN detect and respond to airborne sound.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer choice against that standard:
  • Choice A — Neither eastern brown snakes nor rough-scaled death adders display movement of more than 20 cm in response to airborne sound.: This supports the assumption rather than challenging it — it shows two species failing to detect airborne sound. ✗
  • Choice B — Woma pythons have better overall hearing than do rough-scaled death adders.: This compares hearing ability between species but says nothing about whether either can detect airborne sound. ✗
  • Choice C — Both woma pythons and eastern brown snakes react only to sounds traveling through the ground.: This confirms the assumption by showing two species responding only to ground sound, not airborne sound. ✗
  • Choice D — Woma pythons, eastern brown snakes, and rough-scaled death adders display movement of more than 20 cm in response to both ground and airborne sound waves.: All three species show substantial movement in response to airborne sound, directly disproving the assumption. ✓
That matches Choice D — Woma pythons, eastern brown snakes, and rough-scaled death adders display movement of more than 20 cm in response to both ground and airborne sound waves..
